deep_dive.title: SceneMiner: Identity-Preserving Multi-Task Fine-Tuning for Unified BEV Scene Mining
deep_dive.abstract: Mining hard, safety-critical scenes from driving logs is bottlenecked by the absence of difficulty labels, and no single proxy, collision risk, trajectory ambiguity, or semantic rarity suffices to find such scenes on its own. We present SceneMiner, a unified, camera-only bird's-eye-view pipeline that emits complementary mining signals from a frozen vision-language backbone in a single forward pass, with no LiDAR or radar: a retrieval embedding for text-prompted scenario search, a multi-label scene-tag distribution, and a continuous physics-based risk score (a motion forecast is a byproduct, not a contribution). Building such a multi-head model exposes our central finding, a failure mode we term cross-task interference: adding or upgrading one head shifts a shared activation stream and degrades weight-frozen sibling heads, so freezing parameters alone is insufficient. Our contribution, identity-preserving multi-task fine-tuning, removes this interference by zero-initializing every new sub-module and freezing every parameter that feeds the shared stream. The mining heads are thereby preserved bit-identically while training only ~102k parameters. The tagging head reaches mAP 0.4614 (micro-F1 0.5557) on 20 scene tags by pooling each scene into 32 visual tokens, and the embedding head supports text-prompted retrieval, validated qualitatively. Code is available at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/sceneminer_anonymous-64E5
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: retrieval embedding for text-prompted scenario search to find dynamic events like cut-ins and hard braking
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用文本提示检索嵌入来查找剪切和急刹等动态事件
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用文本提示检索嵌入来查找剪切和急刹等动态事件
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用文本提示检索嵌入来查找剪切和急刹等动态事件
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a multi-task bird's-eye-view pipeline for mining safety-critical driving scenes, with a retrieval embedding enabling text-prompted scenario search, useful for retrieving moments from driving videos based on query text.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对缺乏困难标签导致安全关键场景挖掘瓶颈的问题，提出统一的多任务BEV框架SceneMiner，通过冻结的视觉语言主干在一次前向中输出检索嵌入、场景标签和风险评分。文本提示场景搜索的检索嵌入可直接从驾驶日志中检索如剪入、急刹等动态事件时刻。实验表明该方法无需激光雷达和雷达，有效减少了跨任务干扰，为自动驾驶数据策管和验证提供了高效工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对缺乏困难标签导致安全关键场景挖掘瓶颈的问题，提出统一的多任务BEV框架SceneMiner，通过冻结的视觉语言主干在一次前向中输出检索嵌入、场景标签和风险评分。文本提示场景搜索的检索嵌入可直接从驾驶日志中检索如剪入、急刹等动态事件时刻。实验表明该方法无需激光雷达和雷达，有效减少了跨任务干扰，为自动驾驶数据策管和验证提供了高效工具。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: video moment retrieval based on natural language queries

deep_dive.title: SpikeTAD: Spiking Neural Networks for End-to-End Temporal Action Detection
deep_dive.abstract: Video understanding is a crucial part of computer vision, with numerous application scenarios. With the increasing popularity of mobile devices, an increasing number of efforts are trying to deploy video understanding models on them. However, existing video understanding models are difficult to deploy due to their large size and prohibitive power consumption. Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) have shown bioplausibility and low power advantages over Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), especially on neuromorphic chips which are regarded as essential components of future mobile devices. However, excessively long conversion time-steps and severe performance degradation problems limit their application. To solve the problems above, we explore the application of SNNs on temporal action detection (TAD), which is an important task in video understanding, and propose the first SNN-based end-to-end TAD architecture coined as SpikeTAD. While maintaining extremely low power consumption, SpikeTAD achieves an average mAP of 67.2% in THUMOS14 and 37.42% in ActivityNet-1.3, demonstrating the feasibility of a low-power TAD model. Our code is available at https://github.com/MCG-NJU/SpikeTAD.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: end-to-end temporal action detection using spiking neural networks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用脉冲神经网络进行端到端时序动作检测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用脉冲神经网络进行端到端时序动作检测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用脉冲神经网络进行端到端时序动作检测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Explores spiking neural networks for end-to-end temporal action detection, aiming for low-power, mobile-friendly video understanding.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 鉴于现有视频理解模型庞大且功耗高，难以部署于移动设备，本文探索将脉冲神经网络用于端到端时序动作检测。针对SNN转换时间步过长和性能退化问题，提出SpikeTAD方法。实验表明，该方法在显著降低功耗的同时，在时序动作检测任务上达到了有竞争力的精度，为视频理解模型在移动端的低功耗部署提供了可行路径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 鉴于现有视频理解模型庞大且功耗高，难以部署于移动设备，本文探索将脉冲神经网络用于端到端时序动作检测。针对SNN转换时间步过长和性能退化问题，提出SpikeTAD方法。实验表明，该方法在显著降低功耗的同时，在时序动作检测任务上达到了有竞争力的精度，为视频理解模型在移动端的低功耗部署提供了可行路径。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: methods for temporal action localization in untrimmed videos
